title: CytomX surges on positive data for ‘masked’ ADC in colorectal cancer
summary: Company shares spiked more than 60% after the therapy surpassed Wall Street benchmarks in Phase 1 testing against late-line colorectal tumors.

summary: Two people with paralysis were able to type using a brain-computer interface that decodes attempted finger movement, a new study showed.

summary: Testing revealed weight loss effects some Wall Street analysts believe compare favorably to Lilly’s orforglipron, which regulators could approve within weeks.

title: Sarepta tests new Elevidys safeguards; Sana advances diabetes cell therapy

summary: An experimental obesity pill from Structure Therapeutics helped patients lose significant weight in a mid-stage trial, with a Phase 3 study in the works.

summary: "Watching my mom die of ALS was one of the most awful things I’ve gone through. I don’t want my children to see me suffer like that."

summary: Congress must pass bipartisan legislation to ensure Medicare coverage of breakthrough medical technologies, writes a physician and inventor.

title: STAT+: New technologies promise to transform organ transplantation. But when?
summary: Perfusion technology vastly improves organ quality in transplants, but high costs and coverage questions pose barriers in becoming the standard of care.

summary: It's Oscar season, so we're bringing you a special STATus report on how we'd cover the drug scandal in "The Fugitive" if it were real.

title: Major changes to cardiovascular guidelines suggest taking statins as young as 30
summary: Cardiology groups say adults as young as 30 should assess cholesterol risk and consider treatment to prevent heart attacks and strokes.

summary: The trial clearance for Shanghai-based Unixell is another step forward for the rapidly advancing Chinese biotech ecosystem, which has invested heavily in cell and gene therapy technology.

summary: Evotec will cut 800 employees in its second layoff round since 2024. Elsewhere, Ultragenyx got a needed win and a twice-rejected cell therapy is getting an audience at the FDA.

summary: “We cannot let alarm fatigue silence the response to Kennedy’s and Trump’s destruction,” writes Robert Shpiner.

summary: “Surrealism ought to be part of the care of people living with diseases that cause dementia,” writes Jason Karlawish.

summary: Lilly asked the FDA to step in after claiming that testing it’s done suggests copycat versions of Zepbound may carry “unknown risks.”

title: FDA to unify agency’s ‘fragmented’ safety surveillance system
summary: Combining the disparate databases used to detect potential issues with drugs, vaccines and other products into a single dashboard will enhance their utility and cut costs, the agency said.

summary: More early-stage data show the drug, called salanersen, appeared to have positive effects on neurodegeneration and motor function in patients previously treated with Novartis' Zolgensma.
